title: Gaussian Elimination and Linear Systems
summary: Solve systems of linear equations using Gaussian elimination and row reduction to row echelon form.
content_html:
Gaussian elimination is a systematic method to solve systems of linear equations by transforming the augmented matrix to row echelon form.

Augmented Matrix

The system 2x + y = 5, x − y = 1 is written as augmented matrix [A|b]:

[2  1 | 5]
[1 -1 | 1]

Row Operations (Preserve Solutions)

  1. Swap two rows
  2. Multiply a row by a non-zero scalar
  3. Add a multiple of one row to another

Row Echelon Form (REF)

Goal: make the matrix upper triangular with leading 1s (pivots):

[1  0.5 | 2.5]
[0  1   | 1  ]

Then back-substitute to find all unknowns.

Reduced Row Echelon Form (RREF)

Continue eliminating upward as well, so each pivot column has only the pivot (1) and zeros.

Solution Types

  • Unique solution: n pivots, no contradictions
  • No solution: row like [0 0 | 5] — contradiction
  • Infinitely many: fewer pivots than unknowns (free variables)

exercises.title: Augmented matrix
exercises.prompt: The system x + 2y = 5, 3x − y = 4 as an augmented matrix is:

exercises.title: Valid row operation
exercises.prompt: Which of these is NOT a valid elementary row operation?
options: Multiplying an element (not the whole row) by a scalar
Swapping two rows
Multiplying a row by a non-zero scalar
Adding a multiple of one row to another
exercises.answer: Multiplying an element (not the whole row) by a scalar

exercises.title: No solution indicator
exercises.prompt: Which row in reduced form indicates no solution exists?
options: [0 0 | 5] — leading to 0 = 5

exercises.title: Free variables
options: There are fewer pivot columns than unknowns
There are more equations than unknowns
The determinant is non-zero
All rows have pivots
exercises.answer: There are fewer pivot columns than unknowns

exercises.title: Unique solution
options: The number of pivots equals the number of unknowns
The system has more equations than unknowns
det(A) = 0
There are free variables
exercises.answer: The number of pivots equals the number of unknowns

exercises.title: Row echelon form
exercises.prompt: In row echelon form, each row's leading element (pivot) must be:
options: To the right of the pivot in the row above
In the first column
Equal to 1
The largest element in its column
exercises.answer: To the right of the pivot in the row above

exercises.title: Back substitution
exercises.prompt: After reaching row echelon form, you find the solution by:
options: Back substitution (working from bottom row upward)
Forward substitution
Adding all equations
Taking the determinant
exercises.answer: Back substitution (working from bottom row upward)

exercises.title: RREF difference
exercises.prompt: Reduced Row Echelon Form (RREF) differs from REF by:
options: Also eliminating entries above each pivot (not just below)
Using fractions instead of integers
Having all 1s in the matrix
Only being applicable to square matrices
exercises.answer: Also eliminating entries above each pivot (not just below)

exercises.title: Row operation purpose
exercises.prompt: Why are elementary row operations used in Gaussian elimination?
options: They preserve the solution set of the linear system
They change the solution to be easier
They reduce computation time only
They work only for square matrices
exercises.answer: They preserve the solution set of the linear system

exercises.title: Pivot definition
exercises.prompt: A pivot in row echelon form is:
options: The leftmost non-zero entry in a row
The largest element in a row
Always equal to 1
The entry in the last column
exercises.answer: The leftmost non-zero entry in a row
